message:
skill-loop pass 2/10: simplify build slot conflicts

## Change: collapse build slot conflict predicate

### Equivalence contract

- Inputs covered: active build-slot leases passed through collect_slot_conflicts.

- Ordering preserved: yes; released-lease and same-agent filters still run before the predicate and conflict Vec order is unchanged.

- Tie-breaking: unchanged; active lease iteration order is preserved.

- Error semantics: N/A; no error path changed.

- Laziness: unchanged; same per-entry loop.

- Short-circuit eval: unchanged; request_exclusive=true returns true without reading entry.exclusive, as before.

- Floating-point: N/A.

- RNG / hash order: unchanged.

- Observable side-effects: unchanged; only the same conflicts.push(entry) call remains.

### Verification

- cargo fmt --check

- git diff --check

- cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools build_slots --locked

- cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets

- cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings

- ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/build_slots.rs (no critical findings)

+# Skill Loop Progress
+# Skill: simplify-and-refactor-code-isomorphically
+# Target: /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust
+# Total Passes: 10

+
+## Status: IN PROGRESS - Pass 3 of 10
+
+## Architecture Summary
+- Workspace is a Rust 2024 Cargo workspace with strict layering: `core` owns config/models/errors/metrics; `db` owns SQLite schema/query/cache/search persistence; `storage` owns Git archive and commit coalescing; `tools` owns the MCP tool implementations; `server` owns stdio/HTTP dispatch, TUI/web/ATC/runtime workers; `cli` owns `am`, robot mode, operator commands, gates, E2E, and benchmarks.
+- Runtime entry points are `crates/mcp-agent-mail/src/main.rs` for the `mcp-agent-mail` server binary and `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/main.rs`/`src/lib.rs` for the `am` CLI surface.
+- Tool-call flow is transport -> `mcp-agent-mail-server` JSON-RPC dispatch -> argument normalization/domain observation -> `mcp-agent-mail-tools` -> DB/storage/search/share helpers -> JSON/HTML/TUI/robot renderers.
+- Critical invariants: no unsafe code, no Tokio ecosystem, no broadcast messaging support in `send_message`, no file deletion, no script-based code rewrites, and explicit Agent Mail reservations before edits.
+
+## Missions
+1. Messaging Normalization Candidate: verify and finish the current low-risk Type II helper extraction around agent-name normalization without weakening `broadcast=true` rejection.
+2. Error Propagation and Wrapper Collapse: find simple pass-through `Result`/`Option` plumbing where `?` or direct return removes boilerplate without changing error variants.
+3. CLI Formatting and Output Helpers: reduce repeated formatting or tiny string-construction branches in `am`/robot code while preserving exact output contracts.
+4. Config and Env Parsing Simplification: collapse repeated boolean/list/env parsing shapes in core configuration with tests or existing parser coverage.
+5. Core Model Validation Helpers: simplify duplicated validation helpers in `core` models/setup while preserving user-facing error strings.
+6. DB Query/Test Boilerplate: reduce repeated SQL row decode or test fixture boilerplate in DB code without changing schema/query behavior.
+7. Storage and Archive Path Construction: simplify repeated archive path/artifact construction in storage code while preserving on-disk paths and Git write ordering.
+8. Server Snapshot and Domain Event Formatting: simplify bounded formatter/snapshot helper duplication in server runtime surfaces without touching transport semantics.
+9. Test Harness Duplication: simplify repeated test helper scaffolding in conformance/golden/CLI harnesses without reducing coverage.
+10. Final Rescan and Ledger: rescan for remaining score >= 2 candidates, apply one final safe shrink if found, otherwise record convergence with concrete checks.
+
+## Completed Passes
+### Pass 1 - Messaging Normalization Candidate - 2026-04-25T01:46:00Z
+- Files changed by this pass: none in this checkout.
+- Existing landed code verified: `9ad6c2db` includes the messaging agent-name normalization helper extraction, the `saturating_age_minutes_since` clamp, reconstruct test cleanup, and release lockfile/version bump.
+- Checks performed: worker re-read `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/messaging.rs` around `normalize_agent_name_or_original`, `send_message`, `reply_message`, `fetch_inbox`, `mark_message_read`, and `acknowledge_message`; confirmed invalid-name fallback still returns the original string; confirmed `Vec` order and `Option<Vec<_>>` shape are preserved; confirmed `broadcast=true` rejection remains in both explicit-target conflict and `BROADCAST_DISABLED` paths.
+- Baseline status: workspace baseline was already red before this loop could add changes; `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` through `rch` recorded CLI/core failures and was interrupted after prolonged silence in DB tests. See `refactor/artifacts/2026-04-25-silentoriole-simplify/baseline.md`.
+- Commit: `9ad6c2db` (already at `HEAD` / `origin/main` when pass closed).
+- Verdict: VERIFIED-EXISTING-LANDED.
+
+### Pass 2 - Build Slot Lease Helper Simplification - 2026-04-25T01:57:00Z
+- Files changed: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/build_slots.rs`.
+- Changes: collapsed the local conflict predicate in `collect_slot_conflicts` from a temporary `if request_exclusive { true } else { entry.exclusive }` binding to the equivalent `request_exclusive || entry.exclusive` condition.
+- Preservation notes: released-lease filtering, same-agent filtering, conflict ordering, lease path compatibility, serialized JSON, and branch-change renew/release helpers were untouched.
+- Verification: worker fresh-eyes inspection passed; `cargo fmt --check`; `git diff --check`; `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-tools build_slots --locked`; `cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets`; `cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-tools --all-targets -- -D warnings`; `ubs crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/build_slots.rs` reported no critical findings.

+# Pass 2 - Build Slot Lease Helper Simplification
+
+## Change
+
+`collect_slot_conflicts` now pushes a conflict when
+`request_exclusive || entry.exclusive` instead of first materializing the same
+boolean through a temporary branch.
+
+## Isomorphism Card
+
+- Inputs covered: all active leases passed to `collect_slot_conflicts`.
+- Ordering preserved: yes; the loop order and earlier `continue` filters are
+  unchanged.
+- Tie-breaking: unchanged; conflict order still follows active lease order.
+- Error semantics: N/A; the helper does not return errors.
+- Short-circuit evaluation: equivalent; when `request_exclusive` is true, the
+  old branch returned true without reading `entry.exclusive`, and the new `||`
+  expression does the same.
+- Observable side effects: unchanged; only the same `conflicts.push(entry)` call
+  remains.
+- On-disk compatibility: unchanged; lease-path helpers and JSON serialization
+  were not touched.
+
